sections: HCM-0.4 crate ownership and dependency contract
Stop recommendation superseded only if this packet closes clean

repo_truth: The exact three-file manifest is the complete current review subject. The worktree baseline was cf04accb5e314efec07f1285004474a38da2520c on feat/handbook-contract-membrane and was clean before landing. Only the three packet files changed. Canonical 00-08, canonical 05, code, schemas, validators, HCM-0.6, HCM-0.9, and historical evidence are unchanged. HCM-0.4 is completed dependency evidence; HCM-0.9 is abandoned evidence; the selected non-clean HCM-0.5 handoff is historical/resume evidence only and is not the future selector. The packet is intended to preserve the substantive HCM-0.5 contract/dock design while deleting duplicated orchestration history, counters, and state-machine topology.
allowed_scope: Read and replay the exact three-file manifest before reviewing.
Read only the named authority and directly relevant HCM-0.2/HCM-0.3/HCM-0.4/HCM-0.5 sections needed to validate preservation and consistency.
Run read-only Markdown, link, anchor, fence, portability, Git, schema/example, handoff, and scope checks.
Review the complete three-file packet against the locked simplification contract and return typed findings without editing.
non_goals: No repository edits, staging, commits, new dispatches, global handoff, ledger mutation, or user task hop by the reviewer.

Do not require numbered review identities, review/remediation counters, a review-transition table, historical review topology, Task 7, changes to canonical 07/08, or a generic configurable review-budget framework.
Do not require another artifact, ADR, migration plan, runtime implementation, HCM-0.6 work, HCM-0.9 work, canonical edits, or speculative hardening beyond the locked HCM-0.5 semantics.
Do not reinterpret HCM-0.4 frozen interfaces, make HCM-0.9 authoritative, or treat a historical non-clean handoff as the future design selector.
tasks: Invoke using-agent-skills first, then documentation-and-adrs and code-review-and-quality; use api-and-interface-design only for schema/protocol/interface claims. Remain read-only.
Replay the sorted manifest and aggregate fingerprint, then review the complete current SPEC, plan, and todo rather than only the diff.

Verify no substantive HCM-0.5 semantics were lost or weakened: objective/success; owners; identity/fingerprint/version/lifecycle; claims/applicability; evidence provenance/freshness/cardinality/precedence/Resolution; verdict partition; gate hard-fail; dock manifest; process JSON; implementation/runtime closure; isolation/no-network/timeout/cancellation/refusal/crash/cleanup; validator witness authority; Draft 2020-12 first dock; HCM-0.4-compatible ordinary operations; negative cases; affected sections; proof; non-goals/open runtime gates.

Verify the one execution-entry gate is explicit and consistent: review-clean packet commit -> validated v1.2 context-boundary handoff binding commit/path/fingerprint -> fresh session selects exact handoff -> fresh session validates live repository/packet identity -> canonical design edits may begin. The current non-clean handoff must not be the future selector.

Verify SPEC, plan, and todo are mutually consistent, forward-looking, direct, acyclic, and executable without guessing; every plan task has acceptance criteria, verification, dependencies, and likely files; todo contains only future unchecked work.

Verify generic orchestration is centralized by reference to canonical 07/08 and the packet contains no historical/future numbered review state machine, fixed counters, closed transition table, special handoff-exception task, or copied generic mechanics.

Verify required final-review and closeout invariants remain: full proof before final canonical review; every intended canonical byte including final 00 status bytes in the review manifest; accepted blocker remediation plus different-fresh re-review; no post-CLEAN mutation; no completion from non-clean subject; byte-identical proof/staging; reviewed-slice commit separate from mechanical handoff/ledger commit; no automatic HCM-0.6 start.

Classify each finding with severity Critical, Important, or Suggestion and taxonomy CONTRACT_FAILURE, INTRODUCED_REGRESSION, REQUIRED_IN_SCOPE_SAFETY, PRE_EXISTING, OPTIONAL_HARDENING, or OUT_OF_SCOPE. Include exact file/location, violated locked requirement or canonical authority, evidence, smallest in-scope correction, and missing verification.

Return exactly one provisional verdict: CLEAN, CHANGES_REQUIRED, HUMAN_DECISION_REQUIRED, or EXTERNAL_VALIDATION_REQUIRED. Optional hardening, historical-process reconstruction, extra artifacts, generic control-plane redesign, and prohibited-scope changes are non-blocking unless the locked contract demonstrably requires them.
contracts_and_gates: Blocking threshold: Critical blocks only when in scope or introduced. Important blocks only for CONTRACT_FAILURE, INTRODUCED_REGRESSION, or REQUIRED_IN_SCOPE_SAFETY. Suggestions and PRE_EXISTING/OPTIONAL_HARDENING/OUT_OF_SCOPE never block.
Preserve exact HCM-0.4 operation/transport semantics and preliminary HCM-0.5 substantive design decisions; shortening cannot weaken them.
The packet must contain no normative match for Planning Review number, Continuation Planning Review, Design Review number, Review 5, Remediation number, review budget, or stage-scoped review.
The reviewer is independent and read-only; parent adjudicates findings under the locked contract.
stop_conditions: Return HUMAN_DECISION_REQUIRED if a genuine correction would require prohibited scope or materially different architecture.
Return EXTERNAL_VALIDATION_REQUIRED only when a named external/human-only check remains after automated review.
Do not modify repository state or write orchestration artifacts.
Stop and report if the manifest cannot be replayed or the bounded authority cannot be read.
